page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-176 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 176 vCPUs mapped to 88 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 352 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 146478.58 and a PassMark CPU score of 105096.7. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 458.9 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 190.34 GB/sec for write operations. The hardware excels in cryptographic and vector-based workloads, as evidenced by top-tier scores in PassMark encryption and Geekbench ray tracing. Single-core performance remains in the average tier, indicating that the instance's strength lies in highly parallelized execution.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-highcpu-176 offers high compute density and specialized instruction support without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it highly suitable for compute-heavy workloads that do not require persistent local SSDs. Best-fit workloads include high-throughput database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and parallelized CPU-based LLM inference, where its strong multi-threaded capabilities and AMX instruction support can be fully leveraged.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-176 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 176 vCPUs and 352 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with an x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.7 GHz across 88 physical cores, it includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance does not feature local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, while single-core metrics remain average. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for parallelized, compute-intensive tasks that do not require local storage. It is ideally suited for high-throughput database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized platform featuring 176 vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 176 vCPUs and 352 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high-throughput memory bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPUs, optimizing cost for pure compute needs
Well-suited for database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
